prompt: Write a Rust function named normalize_path_parts(parts: &[&str]) -> Vec<String> that removes empty segments and single dots while preserving normal names. Include one small unit test.
Write a TypeScript function named countByStatus that accepts an array of { status: string } objects and returns a Record<string, number>. Include one tiny example.
Return only compact JSON for three benchmark cases comparing baseline and MTP. Each item needs fields: name, nodes, mtp_enabled, and expected_signal.
List five concrete checks to run before trusting an MTP benchmark result. Use short numbered items.
Explain in two short paragraphs why MTP proposals still need target-model verification before tokens are committed.
A local inference run gets faster TTFT but slower total latency with MTP enabled. Give three likely causes and one measurement for each.
Sketch a practical rollout plan for testing a new speculative decoding path across one-node and two-node serving setups.
Draft a concise README note explaining when to use a smaller quant for local MTP experiments.
